human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for ambulatory-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, attribution and visit-state workflow grounding, privacy and public-reporting escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and cross-industry research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for analytics-reporting-and-disclosure plus cross-industry are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to ambulatory clinic analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, clinician attribution, visit-state terminology, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.
Authority sources cover ambulatory quality reporting, physician claims-processing, registry and eCQM workflow, public-reporting posture, profile-data lineage, and HIPAA minimum-necessary controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, tenant fact gaps, adjacent-lane routing, terminology normalization, and clinician-level disclosure refusal behavior.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; attribution, local KPI variants, and specialty-specific reporting differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
